origin.capability_match: Deploy smart contracts and dApps on Berachain

attack_surface.data_description: Excessive or gas-intensive fee transactions (Asset 16) from BEX (Component 3) to FeeCollector (Component 14).

security_gap.gap_description: If the BEX contract (Component 3), managed by a Protocol (Actor 3), sends an unexpectedly high volume of fee transactions or transactions that cause excessive gas consumption in the FeeCollector (Component 14).

actor_risk.description: If Actor 3 deployed BEX (Component 3), a bug in BEX or malicious intent from Actor 3 could trigger this DoS.

behavioral_risk.rationale: FeeCollector should be robust, but extreme transaction volumes or gas griefing could impact its performance or cost.

assumptions: The BEX dApp (Component 3) is deployed or managed by Actor 3.
BEX (Component 3) might have a bug or be intentionally coded to send excessive/problematic fee transactions to Component 14.
The FeeCollector (Component 14) might be susceptible to gas griefing or performance degradation from high transaction load.

mitigation.strategy: Design the FeeCollector Smart Contract (Component 14) to be gas-efficient in handling fee receipts. Implement monitoring for transaction volumes from fee sources. Potentially add circuit breakers or throttling if a source becomes problematic.

description: If the BEX dApp (Component 3), assumed to be deployed/managed by 'Protocol / Ecosystem Project' (Actor 3), experiences a bug or is maliciously crafted, it could send an excessive volume of dApp Fee transactions (Asset 16 via Data Flow 22) to the FeeCollector Smart Contract (Component 14). This could potentially cause a denial of service or high operational costs for the FeeCollector.

trace_source.matched_capability: Deploy smart contracts and dApps on Berachain
